title: The development of aperiodic neural activity in the human brain
methods: structural MRI grey matter volume extraction
data_sources: Clinical multi-site intracranial EEG recordings (USA hospitals)

data_scope.clinical_context: patients undergoing iEEG monitoring for drug-resistant epilepsy

core_conclusion: Aperiodic (1/f-like) neural activity flattens with age into young adulthood across both association and sensorimotor cortices, challenging models of early sensorimotor maturation. In the prefrontal cortex, attentional state differentially modulates aperiodic slopes in children versus adults; task-based slopes in the middle frontal gyrus mediate age-related gains in episodic memory, directly linking PFC cognitive-control maturation to memory development across the lifespan.
why_it_matters: First large-scale lifespan iEEG mapping of neural noise with 5,691 electrodes across 101 participants; challenges the prevailing view that sensorimotor regions mature early; links a neural-noise biomarker to real-world cognitive performance; integrates structural MRI with electrophysiology for multi-modal developmental inference; fully preregistered and open.
why_nature: Cross-disciplinary scope spanning cognitive neuroscience, developmental psychology, and clinical neurophysiology; exceptionally rare large developmental iEEG cohort; challenges an established paradigm with direct educational and clinical implications; preregistered and open, aligning with Nature journal standards for rigor.
added_value_to_my_research: Urban context (density, green space, noise) as a modulator of PFC aperiodic maturation trajectories — extending health geography to a neural endpoint
Aperiodic slope as an objective wearable cognitive-noise signal to complement social-media/GPS proxies in urban place analysis
SHAP/explainable ML applied to electrode-level predictors of slope heterogeneity — generalizing my GeoAI toolkit to brain-region attribution
Wearable EEG + GPS fusion to link green-space accessibility (2SFCA) to cognitive-noise outcomes at population scale
Digital-access equity as a potential modulator of aperiodic aging curves — strengthening the neural-health argument for broadband accessibility research

related.title: Global urbanicity is associated with brain and behaviour in young people

related.title: Functional brain imaging and population-level visits to urban spaces
related.relation: validates pairing neural imaging data with place-visit data to test urban-environment effects on brain function

related.title: Wearable data link urban green space to physical activity
related.relation: demonstrates sensor-fusion (wearable + GPS) approach for linking urban green-space exposure to health outcomes at population scale

related.title: Discovering cognitive strategies with tiny recurrent neural networks
related.relation: complements this paper by using computational neural models to decode cognitive strategies — applicable to ML-based decoding of aperiodic slope predictors

related.title: Positive association between Internet use and mental health among adults aged ≥50 years in 23 countries
related.relation: provides epidemiological evidence that digital engagement protects older adult cognition, which the aperiodic framework could mechanistically underpin
